fix: handle double Bearer prefix in MCP auth header

Some MCP clients (e.g. VS Code Copilot) add their own 'Bearer '
prefix to the Authorization header value from config, resulting in
'Bearer Bearer <token>'. Now extractBearerToken strips up to two
Bearer prefixes and also accepts raw tokens without any prefix.

feat(prism): v0.3b - 脚本化硬门禁（distill借鉴）+ session物理化

## 核心改动（向 distill skill 借鉴约束驱动机制）

### 新增脚本
- tools/prism-dispatch-check.sh：Dispatch 前置硬门禁
  - 检查对抗角色完整性（redteam/council 必须）
  - 检查家族多样性（redteam 必须 ≥3 家族）
  - 检查 Agent 数量范围
  - 检查问题包 >800 行警告（GPT 截断防护）
  - exit 1 = 物理阻断，不再是文档文字约束
- tools/prism-archive-check.sh：Archive 前置校验
  - 验证 verdict 已写入、quorum 记录存在
  - 验证 index.yaml 已更新
  - 验证 lessons.md 时间戳（是否遗漏沉淀）
  - 已在现有报告上通过测试

### 协议更新
- prism/protocol.md：
  - session_registry 从「运行内存」改为物理文件
    prism/reports/.session-registry.yaml（gitignored）
  - Dispatch 门禁引用 prism-dispatch-check.sh 命令
  - Archive Step 4 引用 prism-archive-check.sh 命令

### 知识沉淀
- knowledge/lessons.md：新增 L-35（约束驱动系统性排错）

## 测试结果（3 场景全部正确）
✅ 合法 redteam（3家族+3对抗角色）→ 通过
✅ 缺少对抗角色 → 拦截
✅ 家族不足（全 claude）→ 拦截

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Infer Agent from AgentPlatform by aggregate name with DefaultAgent fallback (#328)

* Add plan for agent resolution refactoring

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/e6ed61c0-c5ac-4bbc-9cab-5e7276244968

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor adapters to infer Agent by name with Autonomy fallback

Replace direct Agent injection in AgenticCommandHandlerFunctionAdapter and
AgenticEventHandlerFunctionAdapter with runtime agent resolution based on
the aggregate name. If no matching agent is found, fall back to
Autonomy.chooseAndAccomplishGoal() to let Embabel decide.

Remove resolveAgent() from AgenticAggregateRuntimeFactory; pass aggregate
name (String) instead of Agent to adapter constructors.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/e6ed61c0-c5ac-4bbc-9cab-5e7276244968

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add unit tests for agent resolution and Autonomy fallback

Add AgenticCommandHandlerFunctionAdapterTest (12 tests) and
AgenticEventHandlerFunctionAdapterTest (7 tests) covering:
- Exact name match resolution
- Suffix name match resolution
- Autonomy chooseAndAccomplishGoal() fallback
- Exception wrapping for Autonomy failures
- Exact match precedence over suffix match
- resolveAgentByName static helper
- Basic adapter properties

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/e6ed61c0-c5ac-4bbc-9cab-5e7276244968

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: catch ProcessExecutionException specifically, fix test mocks

- Narrow exception catch from generic Exception to ProcessExecutionException
- Use doThrow() for Kotlin final method stubbing in tests
- Provide non-null AgentProcess to ProcessExecutionFailedException constructor

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/e6ed61c0-c5ac-4bbc-9cab-5e7276244968

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix build: use agents() instead of getAgents() in AgenticEventHandlerFunctionAdapter

The code review suggestion commit introduced getAgents() which does not
exist on the AgentPlatform interface. The correct method is agents().
Also restored single-pass agent resolution logic with suffix match
support that was lost in the code review suggestion.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/e6bbbe64-67fe-4997-9046-7e96a9a43702

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix javadoc: remove stale {@link Agent} references in AgenticAggregateRuntimeFactory

The class-level javadoc still referenced {@link Agent} which was no
longer imported after agent resolution moved to the adapters. Updated
the paragraph to describe the current lazy-resolution behavior.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/d46ab258-b06a-4d2a-b1ed-ccc2c1db2aae

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Autonomy fallback with default agent resolution

The Autonomy.chooseAndAccomplishGoal() approach blocks the thread until
completion, causing Kafka consumer timeouts. Replace it with a default
agent fallback: when no agent matches the aggregate name (exact or
suffix), the first available agent from the platform is used instead.

- Remove Autonomy imports and fallback code from both adapters
- Change resolveAgentByName to return Agent directly (not Optional)
- Throw IllegalStateException only when no agents are deployed at all
- Update tests to verify default agent and no-agents-deployed scenarios

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/8cf48950-f35e-46f6-971b-ec135e834c54

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add DefaultAgent in o.e.akces.agentic.embabel package

Create a default Embabel agent annotated with @Agent(planner = UTILITY)
that will always be present on the AgentPlatform. This serves as the
fallback agent when no other agent matches the aggregate name during
agent resolution in the agentic command and event handler adapters.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/1a5b2b84-6e16-4eca-9184-2c6f843ddd89

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use explicit DefaultAgent.AGENT_NAME for fallback instead of first available agent

Updated resolveAgentByName in both adapters to look specifically for the
DefaultAgent by its AGENT_NAME constant instead of using the first
available agent from the platform. Set name="DefaultAgent" on the
@Agent annotation. IllegalStateException is now thrown when no
DefaultAgent is deployed (not just when no agents exist at all).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/16bc6db4-5f7d-4287-b643-ff758d8cf927

Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: replace raw S3 PUT with properly signed rust-s3 client

The previous upload_to_s3() did a raw HTTP PUT without AWS Signature V4,
making access_key/secret_key completely unused. This always 403'd on real
S3 buckets.

Now uses rust-s3 crate with proper authentication:
- Full SigV4 signing via rust-s3 Bucket::put_object_with_content_type()
- Supports S3-compatible services (R2, MinIO, Spaces, etc.)
- New optional settings: s3_custom_domain (CDN URL), s3_path_style (MinIO)
- Proper Content-Type detection preserved
- Correct error reporting with status codes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement vicente-copilot agent instructions

Fills in the full body for the vicente-copilot DevOps agent:
- Identity, scope, and responsibility table (CI/CD, cloud, IaC, GitOps, K8s, Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions)
- Pre-action review checklist (README, infrastructure/README, conventions file)
- Service architecture reference (3 services, ports, DNS, networking rules)
- Verified commands for Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, and GitHub Actions
- Explicit delegation rules for chatbot-builder when app-code changes are needed
- Non-negotiable rules (no secrets in code, no latest tags, never touch contracts/frontend/backend)
- Before-claiming-success verification gate with concrete commands

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(formatter): handle PIVOT ... USING ... ORDER BY without warning (#3)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fixes two bugs in the PIVOT + ORDER BY workaround:

**Bug 1 — multi-statement files only formatted the first tree**

Files with multiple statements (e.g. two `SET VARIABLE` statements
followed by a `PIVOT`) produce multiple parse trees. The workaround
previously only formatted `trees[0]`, leaving preceding statements
unformatted. Now all trees are formatted; the ORDER BY clause is
appended only to the last one (the PIVOT).

**Bug 2 — PIVOT subquery stripped table qualifiers from column
references**

Root cause: DuckDB's generator `TRANSFORMS` maps `exp.Pivot` to a
`preprocess([unqualify_columns])` transform. This runs before generation
and calls `.pop()` on the table/db/catalog parts of every `Column` node
inside the PIVOT, turning `sc.sku_key` into `sku_key`. Even though the
AST was correct, generation always produced unqualified names.

Fix: override `TRANSFORMS` in `JarifyGenerator` to exclude the
`exp.Pivot` entry. The dispatch then falls through to `pivot_sql`
directly — no column modification — and qualifiers are preserved
verbatim.

## Testing

- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/pivot_order_by.expected.sql` regenerated with
correct qualified output
- 46/46 tests passing

Resolves #2

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(tree-agent): support staged allowed types in schema type generation (#26960)

## Description

Adds support for staged allowed types in the tree-agent's TypeScript
schema rendering. Staged types are types that can be read from a node
but cannot yet be written —
they represent a forward-compatible schema upgrade that has not been
fully committed.

Previously, the type generator treated all allowed types uniformly for
reads and writes. With this change:

- Array and map nodes with staged types render as TreeArray<TRead,
TWrite> / TreeMap<TRead, TWrite>, where TRead includes staged types and
TWrite excludes them.
- Object node fields with staged types render as getter/setter pairs
(get foo(): string | number; set foo(value: string);) instead of simple
properties, enforcing
  write-time restrictions.
- Record nodes include a warning comment noting which types are staged
and not writeable.
- The TreeArray and TreeMap prompt documentation is updated to reflect
the dual type parameter pattern.

## Reviewer Guidance

The review process is outlined on [this wiki
page](https://github.com/microsoft/FluidFramework/wiki/PR-Guidelines#guidelines).

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(bot): Pioneer/Harvester 기반 구현 및 OLTP 스키마 정리

Bot 크롤러의 Pioneer/Harvester 모델 기반 구축:

**구현 내용:**
- Pioneer: BFS 기반 사이트 탐색 및 AI 스크립트 생성
- Harvester: 그래프 순회 및 파싱 스크립트 실행
- 도메인 레이어: AIClient, ScriptExecutor 인터페이스 추상화
- 4개 테이블: bot_sites, bot_graph_nodes, bot_graph_edges, bot_scripts

**OLTP 스키마 정리:**
- bot_scripts: 통계 필드 8개 제거 (validation/execution 카운터, 생성 비용)
- bot_sites: 상태 추적 필드 4개 제거 (pioneer 상태, harvest 타임스탬프)
- bot_sources: platform, stats, last_crawled_at 제거
- bot_pioneer_runs, bot_harvest_runs 테이블 삭제
- 운영 메트릭은 로깅/메트릭 시스템으로 분리 (OLTP ≠ OLAP)

**Specs:**
- 5개 capability 추가: bot-graph-management, bot-pioneer-crawler,
  bot-harvester-crawler, bot-script-lifecycle, bot-run-tracking

**Migration:** 000016-000019 (sites, nodes, edges, scripts)

**테스트:** ✅ All bot tests passing, build success

fix(ui): keep sync button fixed width to prevent layout shift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: protect local masternode from gossip UTXO eviction (AV9 re-hijack)

The local node was being repeatedly evicted from its own collateral by
gossip relayed through third-party peers (attacker blacklisted directly
but announces still propagated via 154.217.246.35 et al.).

Root cause: the UTXO-ownership eviction path (line ~656) had no guard
for 'canonical holder IS the local node'. An attacker can forge any
wallet_address in a MasternodeAnnounce to match the collateral UTXO's
output address. If our hot-wallet address differs from the cold-storage
address that funded the collateral, the UTXO check incorrectly evicts us.

Fix: add a canonical_is_local guard BEFORE the stalemate guard. If the
current canonical anchor is the local node, reject all gossip-based
UTXO eviction attempts with a rate-limited warning. Only the local node
itself (same-address reconnect) or an on-chain MasternodeReg tx can
legitimately displace us.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Specify default number of values (9) for breakdown settings in Lens charts (#5810)

The "Number of values" setting across all Lens chart types didn't
document its default. The default was recently increased to 9 (from 3 or
5 depending on dimension position) per elastic/kibana#247015 — already
live in Serverless, coming in Stack 9.4.

- Added version-specific default values using structured bullet lists
with `{applies_to}` tags (not inline) to all 18 occurrences of the
"Number of values" setting across 12 chart type docs: line, bar, area,
table, metric, pie, heat map, waffle, tag cloud, mosaic, treemap, region
map
- Used precise old defaults per dimension position based on Kibana
source code logic (`previousBucketsLength === 0 ? 5 : DEFAULT_SIZE`
where `DEFAULT_SIZE = 3`):
- **5** for 12 primary/sole bucket dimensions: horizontal axis
(line/bar/area/heat-map/mosaic), rows (tables), and single dimensions
(pie/metric/waffle/tag-cloud/treemap/region-map)
- **3** for 6 secondary/breakdown dimensions: breakdown (line/bar/area),
split metrics by (tables), vertical axis (heat-map/mosaic)
- Tutorial/how-to examples that explicitly set specific values (e.g.
`4`, `10`) are unchanged — those are intentional user-chosen values, not
defaults

Example of the change pattern:

```markdown
# Before
- **Number of values**: How many top values to display.

# After (primary dimension)
- **Number of values**: How many top values to display. The default number of values depends on your environment:
  - {applies_to}`serverless: ga` {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.4` Defaults to 9.
  - {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.0-9.3` Defaults to 5.

# After (breakdown/secondary dimension)
- **Number of values**: How many top values to display. The default number of values depends on your environment:
  - {applies_to}`serverless: ga` {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.4` Defaults to 9.
  - {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.0-9.3` Defaults to 3.
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: florent-leborgne <10208282+florent-leborgne@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Florent LB <florent.leborgne@elastic.co>

commit.message:
feat(prism): v0.3a - session 管理 + 追问机制 + Gemini 模型修正

## 变更

### prism/protocol.md
- Dispatch: 新增 session_registry（记录每个 Agent 的 agent_id/role/family/status）
- Collect: 重写为「Schema 完整性追问」流程（优先 write_agent 追问，最多2次，再标 ABSENT）
- 追问禁止项：不得透露其他 Agent 输出 / 引导结论 / 给正确答案暗示

### prism/modes/council.md
- 典型流程重写：Round 2+ 必须用 write_agent(agent_id) 复用原 session，禁止重新 Dispatch 新 Agent
- 新增 Session 复用规则：agent session 全程活跃，超时才标 ABSENT

### knowledge/lessons.md
- 新增 L-34：评审提示词需覆盖「假设但未实现」检查，不只是找错

### .workflow/agent-registry.yaml
- Gemini 模型更新为 gemini-3.1-pro-preview（user-specified，L-11 超时跳过探测）

## 根因（为何之前未发现）
评审提示词问「找设计缺陷」，但 session 复用是「文档假设有但未描述 HOW」——
不在「找错」视野里。L-34 固化了「假设但未实现」专项检查项。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🎨 Palette: Add clear action to SearchInput (#226)

* 🎨 Palette: Add clear action to SearchInput

This change adds a "Clear" button to the `SearchInput` component that appears when the input is not empty.

Key improvements:
- Added `RiCloseLine` button to clear the input immediately.
- The clear action triggers the `onChange` callback immediately, bypassing the debounce delay for better responsiveness.
- Added `aria-label` for accessibility.
- Adjusted input padding to prevent text overlap with the clear button.
- Verified visually with Playwright and ensured the project builds correctly.

Co-authored-by: jakwakwa <20184974+jakwakwa@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

`handleClear` calls `onChange("")` immediately, but the existing debounced `useEffect` will also call `onChange(query)` after the debounce delay when `query` becomes empty. This guarantees a second `onChange("")` emission on every clear, which can cause duplicate work for consumers that trigger side effects directly in `onChange` (e.g., network requests). Consider canceling/skipping the pending debounced emission on clear (e.g., switch to a debounced callback you can `cancel()`, or guard the effect with a ref to ignore the next debounced update after a manual clear).

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🎨 Palette: Refine SearchInput clear button and remove log

- Improved clear button size and padding for better UX.
- Fixed non-functional transition class.
- Removed accidental dev_server.log file.

Co-authored-by: jakwakwa <20184974+jakwakwa@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Feat(checkin): add set_state debug action

Add a set_state entity service that forces the
check-in sensor into any valid state, bypassing
all guard conditions and transition logic.  No HA
bus events are fired.  Clears all tracked event,
transition, and linger fields on invocation.

This enables rapid testing without waiting for
real calendar events to progress through the
state machine.
